fields.definition_from_readme: A static unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to each coal mine by GEM for internal identification purposes.

api_metadata.name: GEM Mine ID
api_metadata.definition: A unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to each coal mine by GEM for internal identification purposes.

fields.definition_from_readme: A static unique numerical identifier assigned to each US coal mine by the US Mine Safety and Health Administration (MSHA) for identification purposes nationwide.

api_metadata.name: MSHA ID
api_metadata.definition: A unique numerical identifier assigned to each US coal mine by the US Mine Safety and Health Administration (MSHA) for identification purposes nationwide.

fields.definition_from_readme: The geographic country or area where a coal mine or proposed coal mine project is located.

api_metadata.name: Country / Area
api_metadata.definition: The geographic country or area where a coal mine or proposed coal mine project is located.

fields.definition_from_readme: The name of a coal mine or proposed coal mine project.

api_metadata.name: Mine Name
api_metadata.definition: The name of a coal mine or proposed coal mine project.
api_metadata.other_rules: Must be unique unless the Mine ID is the same, then we consider it a unit within the mine project parent, duplicates can occur because official Mine names are duplicated within the industry

fields.definition_from_readme: The nickname or alias of a coal mine or proposed coal mine project. AKAs are often used with proposed projects which have not yet been assigned an official name.

api_metadata.name: Mine Name AKAs
api_metadata.null_is_what: Not found, Not applicable, Not yet researched
api_metadata.definition: The nickname or alias of a coal mine or proposed coal mine project. AKAs are often used with proposed projects which have not yet been assigned an official name.

fields.definition_from_readme: "Status" describes the various active/inactive stages of a coal mine or mine project.

value_definitions.name: Operating
value_definitions.definition: A coal mine where coal removal is occurring for commercial purposes. This includes mines that have produced coal within the year, even if operations were temporarily suspended for several months.

value_definitions.name: Proposed
value_definitions.definition: A coal mine project that has not yet begun operation. This includes projects that have been announced (via corporate or government planning documents), projects that are under permitting (including feasibility studies), projects that are under construction, as well as those in test operation.

value_definitions.name: Mothballed
value_definitions.definition: A coal mine that is neither operating nor retired, such as those that are idled or put on "care and maintenance." Such mines could return to operation, but are not currently operating.

value_definitions.name: Cancelled
value_definitions.definition: A proposed coal mine project for which a cancellation announcement has been made by the owning company or government authorities. GEM also assigns a status of "cancelled" to projects when no progress has been observed for 4+ years.

value_definitions.name: Shelved
value_definitions.definition: A proposed coal mine project for which no public updates nor progress has been observed for 2+ years.

value_definitions.name: Closed
value_definitions.definition: A previously operating coal mine that has been permanently closed.

api_metadata.name: Status
api_metadata.definition: "Status" describes the various active/inactive stages of a coal mine or mine project.

values_definitions.Operating: A coal mine where coal removal is occurring for commercial purposes. This includes mines that have produced coal within the year, even if operations were temporarily suspended for several months.
values_definitions.Proposed: A coal mine project that has not yet begun operation. This includes projects that have been announced (via corporate or government planning documents), projects that are under permitting (including feasibility studies), projects that are under construction, as well as those in test operation.
values_definitions.Mothballed: A coal mine that is neither operating nor retired, such as those that are idled or put on "care and maintenance." Such mines could return to operation, but are not currently operating.
values_definitions.Cancelled: A proposed coal mine project for which a cancellation announcement has been made by the owning company or government authorities. GEM also assigns a status of "cancelled" to projects when no progress has been observed for 4+ years.
values_definitions.Shelved: A proposed coal mine project for which no public updates nor progress has been observed for 2+ years.
values_definitions.Retired: A previously operating coal mine that has been permanently closed.

fields.definition_from_readme: "Status detail" describes the mine status on a granular level, such as which planning stage a proposed coal mine project has progressed to, or whether an operating mine is experiencing temporary work stoppages.

value_definitions.name: Announced
value_definitions.definition: A status assigned when a coal mine project has been described in corporate or government plans, but full details remain unknown.

value_definitions.name: Pre-Permit
value_definitions.definition: A status assigned when permit applications (environmental assessments, land use rights, water licenses, etc.) for a coal mine project have been filed, but not yet obtained.

value_definitions.name: Permitted
value_definitions.definition: A status assigned to a coal mine project that has been granted permit approvals, but has not yet begun construction.

value_definitions.name: Construction
value_definitions.definition: A status assigned when a coal mine project has begun construction of its mining infrastructure and facilities.

value_definitions.name: Test Operation
value_definitions.definition: A status assigned when a coal mine project begins coal removal, but not yet for commercial purposes.

value_definitions.name: Care and Maintenance
value_definitions.definition: A status assigned when an operating mine temporarily closes, but there is potential to recommence operations at a later date. During the care and maintenance period, coal production stops but the mine site is still managed by minimal staff to ensure it remains in a safe and stable condition.

api_metadata.name: Status Detail
api_metadata.definition: "Status detail" describes the mine status on a granular level, such as which planning stage a proposed coal mine project has progressed to, or whether an operating mine is experiencing temporary work stoppages.

values_definitions.Announced: A status assigned when a coal mine project has been described in corporate or government plans, but full details remain unknown.
values_definitions.Exploration: A status assigned when the economic viability of a coal mine project is being explored. During the exploration stage, information is gathered about a potential mine site's characteristics via geological surveys, pre-feasability studies, and the drilling of boreholes.
values_definitions.Pre-Permit: A status assigned when permit applications (environmental assessments, land use rights, water licenses, etc.) for a coal mine project have been filed, but not yet obtained.
values_definitions.Permitted: A status assigned to a coal mine project that has been granted permit approvals, but has not yet begun construction.
values_definitions.Construction: A status assigned when a coal mine project has begun construction of its mining infrastructure and facilities.
values_definitions.Test Operation: A status assigned when a coal mine project begins coal removal, but not yet for commercial purposes.
values_definitions.Care and Maintenance: A status assigned when an operating mine temporarily closes, but there is potential to recommence operations at a later date. During the care and maintenance period, coal production stops but the mine site is still managed by minimal staff to ensure it remains in a safe and stable condition.

fields.definition_from_readme: Refers to the type of coal mine project: expansion, extension, or new.

value_definitions.name: New
value_definitions.definition: A "new" mine is a greenfield mine planned to be developed in an area where there was no previous mining activity.

value_definitions.name: Expansion
value_definitions.definition: An "expansion" is when an existing mine seeks to increase its existing coal capacity.

value_definitions.name: Extension
value_definitions.definition: An "extension" is when an existing mine seeks to mine new areas to extend its mine life.

value_definitions.name: Resource Consolidation and Restructuring
value_definitions.definition: A mine that has been established, expanded, or reorganized through the consolidation of fragmented mining rights, resources, assets, or production capacity. This type of asset is predominantly found in China.

api_metadata.name: Project Type
api_metadata.definition: Refers to the type of coal mine project: expansion, extension, or new.

values_definitions.New: A "new" mine is a greenfield mine planned to be developed in an area where there was no previous mining activity.
values_definitions.Expansion: An "expansion" is when an existing mine seeks to increase its existing coal capacity.
values_definitions.Extension: An "extension" is when an existing mine seeks to mine new areas to extend its mine life.

fields.definition_from_readme: Refers to proposed coal mine projects that will be developed in stages, often with initial development followed by subsequent expansions over time (e.g., Phase I, II, III).

api_metadata.name: Project Phase
api_metadata.definition: Refers to proposed coal mine projects that will be developed in stages, often with initial development followed by subsequent expansions over time (e.g., Phase I, II, III).

fields.definition_from_readme: The immediate owner of a coal mine or coal mine project who is responsible for the mining of the coal. The number in brackets next to the owning entity's name denotes the percentage of shares they hold. If an individual rather than a company owns the mine, this is indicated by the term "natural person(s)."

api_metadata.name: Owners
api_metadata.definition: The immediate owner of a coal mine or coal mine project who is responsible for the mining of the coal. The number in brackets next to the owning entity's name denotes the percentage of shares they hold. If an individual rather than a company owns the mine, this is indicated by the term "natural person(s)."

api_metadata.name: Owners GEM Entity ID
api_metadata.definition: Unique identifier assigned by the GEM's Global Energy Ownership Tracker team. Renamed from 'Owner GEM Entity ID' in the May-2026 release; format also changed from 'E12345 [100%]' to 'E12345' (ownership percentages are now exclusively in the Global Energy Ownership Tracker, not denormalized into this cell).

fields.definition_from_readme: The highest-level owner of a coal mine or coal mine project. The number in brackets next to the owning entity's name denotes the percentage of shares they hold. If an individual rather than a company owns the mine, this is indicated by the term "natural person(s)."

api_metadata.name: Parent Company
api_metadata.definition: The highest-level owner of a coal mine or coal mine project. The number in brackets next to the owning entity's name denotes the percentage of shares they hold. If an individual rather than a company owns the mine, this is indicated by the term "natural person(s)."

api_metadata.name: Parent GEM Entity ID
api_metadata.definition: A static unique identifier assigned by GEM's Global Energy Ownership Tracker (GEOT) to the highest-level parent owner of a coal mine.

fields.definition_from_readme: The geographic country or area where a coal mine or coal mine project’s parent company is headquartered in.

value_definitions.name: Owner/Parent GEM Entity ID
value_definitions.definition: A static unique identifier assigned by GEM's Global Energy Ownership Tracker team for internal ownership identification purposes.

api_metadata.name: Company HQs
api_metadata.definition: The geographic country or area where the parent company is headquartered in.

fields.definition_from_readme: The maximum amount of coal a mine or mine project is engineered to produce, expressed in millions of tonnes per year.

api_metadata.name: Capacity (Mtpa)
api_metadata.definition: The maximum amount of coal a mine or mine project is engineered to produce, expressed in millions of tonnes per year.

fields.definition_from_readme: The actual amount of coal produced by a mine, expressed in millions of tonnes per year.

api_metadata.name: Production (Mtpa)
api_metadata.definition: The amount of coal produced by a mine, expressed in millions of tonnes per year.

fields.definition_from_readme: The calendar year (or financial year, depending on the country) during which the cited coal production occurred. (If coal output for the current year is unavailable, data for the most recently available year is given.)

api_metadata.name: Year of Production
api_metadata.definition: The year during which the cited coal production occurred. (If coal output for the current year is unavailable, data for the most recently available year is given.)

fields.definition_from_readme: The actual amount of coal produced by a US mine, expressed in millions of short tons per year. This value applies to US coal mines only.

api_metadata.name: Coal Output (Annual, Mst)
api_metadata.definition: The amount of coal produced by a US mine, expressed in millions of short tons per year. This value applies to US coal mines only.

fields.definition_from_readme: The classification of a coal mine or coal mine project based upon the mining method it uses to extract coal.

value_definitions.name: Surface
value_definitions.definition: A coal mine or coal mine project that extracts coal from shallow seams near the earth’s surface.

value_definitions.name: Underground
value_definitions.definition: A coal mine or coal mine project that extracts coal from deep below the earth’s surface via shafts or tunnels. Also known as “UG” mines.

value_definitions.name: Underground & Surface
value_definitions.definition: A coal mine or coal mine project that extracts coal from both underground and surface methods.

api_metadata.name: Mine Type
api_metadata.definition: The classification of coal mines by mining method, including "Surface," "Underground," and combined "Underground & Surface" facilities.
api_metadata.other_notes: should potentially convert from string to array, since there are two types, but mines can be either or both

fields.definition_from_readme: The specific technique used to extract coal from the earth.

value_definitions.name: Open Pit
value_definitions.definition: A surface mining method that extracts coal from open pits near ground level. Also known as “open cast” mining.

value_definitions.name: Strip
value_definitions.definition: A surface mining method where the overlying vegetation, rock, and soil (overburden) is removed in order to reach the underlying coal seam, which usually lies a few feet beneath the surface.

value_definitions.name: Mountaintop Removal
value_definitions.definition: A surface mining method that extracts coal at the summit or summit ridge of a mountain, often by using explosives to remove their “tops” and expose underlying coal seams.

value_definitions.name: Board and Pillar
value_definitions.definition: An underground mining method in which seams of coal are partially mined, leaving large pillars of coal intact to support the overlying layers of rock. Also known as “room and pillar.”

value_definitions.name: Longwall
value_definitions.definition: An underground mining method in which a large “wall” of coal is progressively sheared off.

value_definitions.name: Mixed
value_definitions.definition: Indicates a coal mine or coal mine project uses more than one mining method.

api_metadata.name: Mining Method
api_metadata.definition: The specific technique used to extract coal from the earth.

values_definitions.Open Pit: also known as “Open Cast”, refers to mining directly on the ground surface
values_definitions.Strip: is a type of surface mining where the overlying vegetation, rock, and soil (overburden) is removed in order to reach the underlying coal seam, which usually lies a few feet beneath the surface.
values_definitions.Mountain Top Removal: is a mining practice where the tops of mountains are removed, exposing the seams of coal. It is a form of surface mining at the summit or summit ridge of a mountain.
values_definitions.Board and Pillar: also known as “Room and Pillar”, it’s a method of underground mining. In this method, seams of coal are mined partially, leaving large pillars of coal intact to support the overlying layers of rock
values_definitions.Longwall: is a type of underground mining method used to extract coal from a seam that is too deep for open-pit mining

fields.definition_from_readme: The total surface area of the coal mining operation, including surface facilities, access roads, overburden dumps, and the underground or open-pit mine footprint.

api_metadata.name: Mine Size (Km2)
api_metadata.definition: The total surface area of the mining covered by the coal mining operation, including surface facilities, access roads, overburden dumps, and the underground or open-pit mine footprint.

fields.definition_from_readme: The vertical distance between the ground surface and the coal seam that is being mined.

api_metadata.name: Mine Depth (m)
api_metadata.definition: The vertical distance from the ground surface down to the coal seam that is being mined.

fields.definition_from_readme: Indicates the confidence level in the reported depth of the coal mine. "Exact" means the depth is directly stated in a reliable source, while "Estimated" means it is inferred from averages at the national level in a variety of industry and government reports.
fields.notes_from_readme: For more details, please refer to GEM’s methodology wiki page on estimating methane emissions from coal mines.

api_metadata.name: Depth Accuracy
api_metadata.definition: Indicates the confidence level in the reported depth of the coal mine. "Exact" means the depth is directly stated in a reliable source, while "Estimated" means it is inferred from averages at the national level in a variety of industry and government reports.

fields.definition_from_readme: The total number of personnel employed by a coal mining operation, including those directly involved in extraction and those supporting operations.

api_metadata.name: Workforce Size
api_metadata.definition: The total number of personnel employed by a coal mining operation, including both direct employees and contractors working on-site.

fields.definition_from_readme: Indicates the accuracy level of workforce data. "Exact" means the workforce size is explicitly reported in data sources, while "Estimated" refers to figures generated using GEM’s machine learning model when official data is unavailable.
fields.notes_from_readme: For more details, please refer to GEM's wiki page on estimating coal mine workforce size.

api_metadata.name: Workforce Accuracy
api_metadata.definition: Indicates the accuracy level of workforce data. "Exact" means the workforce size is explicitly reported in data sources, while "Estimated" refers to figures generated using GEM’s machine learning model when official data is unavailable.

fields.definition_from_readme: The classification of coal based on its physical and chemical properties, particularly its carbon content, calorific value, moisture, volatile matter, and ash content. Global Coal Mine Tracker uses British coal types (lignite, subbituminous, bituminous, anthracite).

api_metadata.name: Coal Type
api_metadata.definition: The classification of coal based on its physical and chemical properties, particularly its carbon content, calorific value, moisture, volatile matter, and ash content. Global Coal Mine Tracker use British coal type (lignite, subbituminous, bituminous, anthracite).

fields.definition_from_readme:
The classification of coal based on its  reported or intended end use, as indicated by coal type, stated use, or project design information.:
- Thermal Coal: Used for power generation or industrial heating.
- Metallurgical Coal (Coking Coal): Used for steelmaking and other industrial processes.
- Chemical Coal: Coal used primarily as a feedstock in coal-to-chemicals processes, which convert coal into gaseous, liquid, or solid fuels and chemical products, particularly in China. In the GCMT, this category includes coal-to-liquids and coal-to-natural gas.

api_metadata.name: Coal Grade
api_metadata.definition: The classification of coal based on its primary use: -Thermal Coal: Used for power generation or industrial heating. -Metallurgical Coal (Coking Coal): Used for steelmaking and other industrial processes.

fields.definition_from_readme: The proportion (expressed as a percentage) of metallurgical coal within the "Thermal & Met" classification under Coal Grades for operating coal mines. Applies to mines that produce a blend of “Thermal & Met” coal only.

api_metadata.name: Percentage of Met Coal
api_metadata.definition: The proportion of metallurgical coal within the "Thermal & Met" classification under Coal Grades for opearting coal mines.

fields.definition_from_readme: The proportion (expressed as a percentage) of thermal coal within the "Thermal & Met" classification under Coal Grades for operating coal mines. Applies to mines that produce a blend of “Thermal & Met” coal only.

api_metadata.name: Percentage of Thermal Coal
api_metadata.definition: The proportion of thermal coal within the "Thermal & Met" classification under Coal Grades for operating coal mines.

fields.definition_from_readme: Indicates the confidence level in the accuracy of the information provided. A percentage split is marked as "Exact" when it is explicitly stated in the data source, and "Estimated" when it is based on the researcher’s interpretation or experience drawn from available sources. If no data is found, a 50/50 split is assumed and this default is also classified as “Estimated”. Applies to “Thermal & Met” coal mines only.

api_metadata.name: Accuracy Rating
api_metadata.definition: Indicates the confidence level in the accuracy of the information provided. A percentage split is marked as "Exact" when it is explicitly stated in the data source, and "Estimated" when it is based on the researcher’s interpretation or experience drawn from available sources.

fields.definition_from_readme: The year in which the percentage breakdown of coal grade was reported or documented. Applies to “Thermal & Met” coal mines only.

api_metadata.name: Reported Year of Percentage Split
api_metadata.definition: The year in which the percentage breakdown of coal grade was reported or deocumented.

fields.definition_from_readme: The total amount of economically recoverable or minable coal. (This includes the measured coal quantities that are sufficiently "proven" plus indicated coal quantities that are "probable.")
fields.notes_from_readme: Please see GEM's wiki on coal reserves for details.

api_metadata.name: Total Reserves (Proven and Probable, Mt)
api_metadata.definition: The combined amount of economically recoverable or minable coal.

fields.definition_from_readme: The year in which the total coal reserves of the mine were reported or evaluated.

api_metadata.name: Year of Total Reserves Recorded
api_metadata.definition: The year in which the total coal reserves of the mine were reported or evaluated.

fields.definition_from_readme: The total estimated volume of a coal deposit in the ground. Also known as “geological reserves” in some countries.

api_metadata.name: Total Resource (Inferred, Indicated, Measured)
api_metadata.definition: The total estimated volume of a coal deposit in the ground

fields.definition_from_readme: A measure of how long a coal reserve will last at the current annual rate of production.

api_metadata.name: Reserve to Production Ratio (R/P)
api_metadata.definition: A measure of how long a coal reserve will last at the current annual rate of production.

fields.definition_from_readme: The year when the mine commenced its commercial operations.

api_metadata.name: Opening Year
api_metadata.definition: The year when the mine commenced its commercial operations.

fields.definition_from_readme: The year in which the coal mine is scheduled to permanently close or did close in, if it has already ceased operations.

api_metadata.name: Closing Year
api_metadata.definition: The year in which the coal mine is scheduled to close or has already ceased operations.

fields.definition_from_readme: The expected operational lifespan of a mine, expressed in years, based on the currently known and economically recoverable reserves, and the planned annual production rate.

api_metadata.name: Reported Life of Mine
api_metadata.definition: The expected operational lifespan of a mine, expressed in year, based on the currently known and economically recoverable reserves, and the planned annual production rate.

fields.definition_from_readme: The year in which the life of mine is reported or evaluated.

api_metadata.name: Reported Year of Mine Life
api_metadata.definition: The year in which the life of mine is reported or evaluated.

fields.definition_from_readme: The coalfield to which a coal mine belongs.

api_metadata.name: Coalfield
api_metadata.definition: Coalfield to which a coal mine belongs.

api_metadata.name: Location (Non-ENG)
api_metadata.definition: The non-English name of the closest city/town to a coal mine or proposed coal mine project.

fields.definition_from_readme: The location of the coal mine or coal mine project expressed in decimal degrees, assumed to use the WGS84 coordinate system (as in Google Maps).

api_metadata.name: Latitude
api_metadata.definition: Location of the project expressed in decimal degrees, assumed to use the WGS84 coordinate system (as in Google Maps).

fields.definition_from_readme: The location of the coal mine or coal mine project expressed in decimal degrees, assumed to use the WGS84 coordinate system (as in Google Maps).

api_metadata.name: Longitude
api_metadata.definition: Location of the project expressed in decimal degrees, assumed to use the WGS84 coordinate system (as in Google Maps).

fields.definition_from_readme: Indicates whether the location is "Approximate" or "Exact". Location is "Approximate" based on using nearest city or inferred from contextual information in the data source. Location is "Exact" when explicitly provided in the source materials.

api_metadata.name: Location Accuracy
api_metadata.definition: Indicates whether the location is "Approximate" or "Exact". Location is "Approximate" based on using nearest city or inferred from contextual information in the data source. Location is "Exact" when explicitly provided in the source materials.

top_values: Shandong Yankuang International Coking Co., Ltd.；No. 1 International Avenue, Yanzhou District, Jining City, Shandong Province
fields.definition_from_readme: The main end user or receiving entity of the coal produced by the mine. This may include coal power plants, steel plants, chemical plants, export terminals, or industrial facilities that rely on coal as a primary source.

value_definitions.name: Primary Consumer, DestinationName
value_definitions.definition: The name of the end user or receiving entity of the coal. The project name should match exactly with those documented by GEM to ensure consistency wherever possible.

api_metadata.name: Primary Consumer, Destination
api_metadata.definition: The main end user or receiving entity of the coal produced by the mine. This may include coal power plants, steel plants, export terminals, or industrial facilities that rely on coal as a primary source.

api_metadata.name: Primary Consumer, Destination Name
api_metadata.definition: The name of the end user or receiving entity of the coal. The project name should match exactly with those documented by GEM to ensure consistency. Renamed from 'Coal Plant, Steel Plant, Terminal' in the May-2026 release; same field, broader scope (any primary consumer/destination, not just plants/terminals).

fields.definition_from_readme: GEM's Wiki URLs of end user or receiving entity of the coal
fields.notes_from_readme: Previously listed as “Coal Plant, Steel Plant, Terminal GEM Wiki” for this column

api_metadata.name: Primary Consumer, Destination GEM Wiki
api_metadata.definition: GEM's Wiki URLs of end user or receiving entity of the coal. Renamed from 'Coal Plant, Steel Plant, Terminal GEM Wiki' in the May-2026 release.

fields.definition_from_readme: The gas-level rating of a coal mine is a classification system that indicates the methane content and gas-related risk level of a coal mine.
fields.notes_from_readme: In this release, this indicator is available only for coal mines in China, where mines are typically classified into three levels: Low-gas, High-gas, and Outburst. For a detailed explanation of the methodology, please visit the wiki page here.

api_metadata.name: Gas-level Rating
api_metadata.definition: The gas-level rating of a coal mine is a classification system that indicates the methane content and gas-related risk level of a coal mine.

fields.definition_from_readme: The year in which the gas-level rating of the coal mine was appraised or officially reported.

api_metadata.name: Gas-level Rating Appraisal Year
api_metadata.definition: The year in which the gas-level rating of the coal mine was appraised or officially reported.

fields.field_name: Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes CH4)
fields.definition_from_readme: The amount of methane emissions from a coal mine as reported by an official source or company reports, expressed in thousands of metric tonnes per year.
fields.notes_from_readme: Column name changed from "Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes per year)" to "Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes CH4) "

api_metadata.name: Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes CH4)
api_metadata.definition: The amount of methane emissions from a coal mine as reported by an official source or company reports, expressed in thousands of metric tonnes per year.

fields.field_name: Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes CO2 equivalent)
fields.definition_from_readme: The amount of methane emissions from a coal mine as reported by an official source or company reports, expressed in thousands of metric tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalents (CO₂e) per year.

api_metadata.name: Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes CO2 equivalent)
api_metadata.definition: The amount of methane emissions from a coal mine as reported by an official source or company reports, expressed in thousands of metric tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalents (CO₂e) per year.
api_metadata.unit_name_long: thousand tonnes CO2 equivalent per year

fields.field_name: Year of Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions
fields.definition_from_readme: The year in which the coal mine methane emissions were reported or released.

api_metadata.name: Year of Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions
api_metadata.definition: The year in which the coal mine methane emissions were reported or released.
associated_field: Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes CH4)
Reported Coal Mine Methane Emissions (thousand tonnes CO2 equivalent)

fields.definition_from_readme: Indicates the official source (national government data or company report) where the reported coal mine methane figures were found.

api_metadata.name: Reference Type
api_metadata.definition: Indicates the official source (national government data or company report) where the reported coal mine methane figures were found.

fields.definition_from_readme: The identifier assigned by the reporting agency to the coal mine associated with the reported methane emissions.

api_metadata.name: Source ID
api_metadata.definition: The identifier assigned by the reporting agency to the coal mine associated with the reported methane emissions.

fields.field_name: Has mine boundary and methane emissions sources data
fields.definition_from_readme: A “Yes” in this field indicates that a coal mine boundary/boundaries and potential sources of coal mine methane have been identified for this GCMT asset.
fields.notes_from_readme: See methodology wiki page here for more information

fields.definition_from_readme: A “Yes” in this field indicates that the GCMT asset has been associated with a remotely sensed methane plume detailed in the Global Methane Emitters Tracker. Yes* refers to the one mine (Mandalong Coal Mine) which had methane plumes associated with it in the December 2024 briefing which were not included in the version of GMET public at the time due to minor differences in query methodology.
fields.notes_from_readme: Some mines may have multiple plumes from different dates/times of observation associated with them. See the wiki page for each GCMT asset for details and download the full Global Methane Emitters Tracker here.

api_metadata.name: Has associated plume data
api_metadata.definition: A “Yes” in this field indicates that the GCMT asset has been associated with a remotely sensed methane plume detailed in the Global Methane Emitters Tracker. Yes* refers to the one mine (Mandalong Coal Mine) which had methane plumes associated with it in the December 2024 briefing which were not included in the version of GMET public at the time due to minor differences in query methodology.

fields.definition_from_readme: A “Yes” in this field indicates that the GCMT asset has been associated with a known coal mine methane mitigation project (e.g., pre-mining drainage, flaring, ventilation air methane destruction, utilization, etc.).
fields.notes_from_readme: Some mitigation projects span multiple mines, and some mines are associated with multiple mitigation projects. See this companion dataset for more details, and methodology wiki page here.

api_metadata.name: Has associated CMM mitigation data
api_metadata.definition: A “Yes” in this field indicates that the GCMT asset has been associated with a known coal mine methane mitigation project (e.g., pre-mining drainage, flaring, ventilation air methane destruction, utilization, etc.).

fields.field_name: GEM Coal Mine Methane Emissions Estimate (MCM/yr)
fields.definition_from_readme: An estimate of annual methane emissions from a coal mine calculated by GEM, expressed in million cubic meters per year (MCM/yr). This estimate is based on the Model for Calculating Coal Mine Methane (MC2M).
fields.notes_from_readme: Due to limited production data for Chinese coal mines, CMM emissions are estimated using the latest annual capacity utilization rate—72.8% for the coal mining and washing industry—as reported by the National Bureau of Statistics of China in 2024.

api_metadata.name: GEM Coal Mine Methane Emissions Estimate (MCM/yr)
api_metadata.definition: An estimate of annual methane emissions from a coal mine calculated by GEM, expressed in million cubic meters per year (MCM/yr). This estimate is based on the Model for Calculating Coal Mine Methane (MC2M).

fields.field_name: GEM Coal Mine Methane Emissions Estimate (M tonnes/yr)
fields.definition_from_readme: An estimate of annual methane emissions from a coal mine, expressed in million metric tonnes per year.

api_metadata.name: GEM Coal Mine Methane Emissions Estimate (M tonnes/yr)
api_metadata.definition: An estimate of annual methane emissions from a coal mine, expressed in million metric tonnes per year.

api_metadata.name: Methane Gas Content (m^3/tonne)
api_metadata.definition: Methane Gas Content (m^3/tonne)

api_metadata.name: Methane Emissions Factor
api_metadata.definition: Methane Emissions Factor

fields.definition_from_readme: An estimate of coal mine methane emissions, expressed in carbon dioxide equivalents (CO₂e) over a 20-year time horizon.
fields.notes_from_readme: Starting in 2022, the Global Coal Mine Tracker adopted the latest conversion factor from the IPCC’s Sixth Assessment Report (2022), using a 20-year Global Warming Potential (GWP) of 82.5.

api_metadata.name: CMM Emissions (CO2e 20 years)
api_metadata.definition: An estimate of coal mine methane emissions, expressed in carbon dioxide equivalents (CO₂e) over a 20-year time horizon.
api_metadata.unit_name_long: carbon dioxide equivalents over a 20-year time horizon.

fields.definition_from_readme: An estimate of coal mine methane emissions, expressed in carbon dioxide equivalents (CO₂e) over a 100-year time horizon.
fields.notes_from_readme: Starting in 2022, the Global Coal Mine Tracker adopted the latest conversion factor from the IPCC’s Sixth Assessment Report (2022), using a 100-year Global Warming Potential (GWP) of 29.8.

api_metadata.name: CMM Emissions (CO2e 100 years)
api_metadata.definition: CMM Emissions (CO2e 100 years)
api_metadata.unit_name_long: carbon dioxide equivalents over a 20-year time horizon.
